official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, and decode the chunks_biomes chunkBiomeData list body
Confirm the official per-chunk element body shape while keeping this fixture empty and context-free
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound chunks_biomes frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns chunks_biomes immediately after chunk_batch_start.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the chunks_biomes id to ClientboundChunksBiomesPacket.
The official chunks_biomes body is a VarInt-prefixed chunkBiomeData list; each non-empty element is ChunkPos plus byte array, but this fixture uses list count 0.

The empty-list fixture uses the official ClientboundChunksBiomesPacket(List<ChunkBiomeData>) constructor, so it does not require an initialized Level, LevelChunk, biome registry/palette contents, chunk storage, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded list counts, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.

This contract does not prove non-empty chunk biome serialization, biome palette contents, initialized LevelChunk extraction, chunk/world hydration, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer non-empty chunk biome serialization, biome palette contents, initialized LevelChunk extraction, chunk/world hydration, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
